title: Configuration
description: howl.config.ts, the Howl app, dev.ts and the HowlBuilder pipeline

blocks.text: A project is configured by three files, each with one job:

rows: State interface, roles, defineConfig → { defineApi, config }
new Howl(), engine registration, middleware, fsApiRoutes/fsClientRoutes
HowlBuilder: esbuild, engine plugin, Tailwind, http-client generation

blocks.text: Your State type and role union live here. defineConfig binds them once and returns a pre-typed defineApi (no per-file generics) plus a config for app.fsApiRoutes().

rows: The valid role union for this app (required).
Auth gate run before every role-protected endpoint. Return a Response to deny, return nothing to allow.
Rate limit applied to every endpoint unless overridden per-route.
Response cache adapter. Defaults to in-memory LRU.
Adapter used only for rate-limit counters. Must be shared across instances in multi-instance deploys.
Resolves the per-user key for rate limits and per-user cache entries (e.g. ctx.state.userContext?.id).

blocks.text: When getRateLimitIdentifier is unset or returns undefined, rate limiting falls back to the client IP and response caching is skipped on role-protected routes — without a per-user key a shared cache entry could leak one user's response to another.
Howl is one class; builder methods return this. Construct with HowlOptions, register an engine, add middleware and routes, export it.

rows: Map of registered render engines, e.g. { vue: vueEngine() } or { react: reactEngine() }.
Run mode. The builder sets this for you (development under dev, production under build).
Path prefix every route is mounted under.
Enable the built-in HowlLogger (timestamp + PID on console output).

blocks.text: app.configure(fn) lets you group setup into a callback and may be awaited if the callback is async — handy for opening a DB connection before registering middleware.
HowlBuilder wraps esbuild with CSS Modules, automatic apis/ crawling, and per-engine page compilation. One file runs both dev (builder.listen) and build (builder.build) — branch on Deno.args.

rows: Lazy loader for the app instance. Accepts the Howl instance or a { app } module shape.
Build output directory. Defaults to _howl; the scaffolder uses dist.
Server entry path; apis/ is resolved relative to its directory.
Client entry (e.g. ./client/pages/_app.tsx); pages crawl from the client root.
esbuild plugins. Always include the engine plugin (vuePlugin() / reactPlugin()).
Import alias map passed straight to esbuild.

blocks.text: The engine's esbuild plugin must be passed in plugins — it owns the .vue / .tsx page extensions and compiles them. The render engine (vueEngine/reactEngine) goes in the Howl constructor; the build plugin (vuePlugin/reactPlugin) goes in HowlBuilder. They are two halves of the same engine.
